message:
fix: correct 3 bugs in watch auto-fallback implementation

1. jq query: was querying nonexistent .throttled_runs field; now
   correctly extracts from .queued_runs and .stuck_runs arrays

2. Flag order: --non-interactive is a global flag that must come
   before the subcommand, not after it

3. Tool name: fallback command expects tool name (e.g., "ntm") not
   full repo path (e.g., "Dicklesworthstone/ntm")

Also simplified SCP test cleanup in test_native_build_e2e.sh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-sfn","title":"E2E: dsr health (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr health (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify host health checks run against real local host data and return schema-valid output.

## Approach
- Use temp hosts.yaml with one local host definition.
- Run `dsr health` and `dsr health check <host>`.

## Logging
- Record hosts config used.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and cache files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Local host reports healthy (or actionable warnings).
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.
- [ ] Cache behavior does not hide errors.","notes":"E2E test created: scripts/tests/e2e_health.sh - covers all acceptance criteria. Blocked by bd-1jt.2 (Theme 2) per dependency graph.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:25.172328917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:58:43.827410233Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:58:43.827082896Z","close_reason":"E2E test e2e_health.sh validates: local host healthy, JSON schema valid, cache behavior correct. Blocker bd-1jt.2 now closed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sfn","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:54.252052939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sfn","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:56.637276950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-v78","title":"Unit tests for toolchain_detect.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for toolchain_detect.sh Module

## Module Overview
toolchain_detect.sh provides safe toolchain detection for installers:
- `toolchain_detect` - Detect specific toolchain (rust/go/bun/node)
- `toolchain_detect_all` - Detect all supported toolchains
- `toolchain_ensure` - Verify toolchain available
- `toolchain_install` - Install if missing (with consent)
- `_tc_version_ge` - Compare semver versions

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Detection Tests
- Detect Rust via `rustc --version`
- Detect Go via `go version`
- Detect Bun via `bun --version`
- Detect Node via `node --version`
- Handle missing toolchain gracefully

### 2. Version Comparison Tests
- 1.70.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.71.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.69.0 >= 1.70.0 → false
- Handle pre-release versions (1.70.0-beta)
- Handle missing version string

### 3. Platform Detection Tests
- Detect linux/amd64 correctly
- Detect darwin/arm64 correctly
- Detect windows/amd64 correctly
- Handle unknown platform

### 4. Installation Tests (with mocks)
- Prompt for consent in interactive mode
- Skip prompt in non-interactive mode
- Never overwrite existing installation
- Log installation commands (dry-run)

### 5. Ensure Tests
- Pass when toolchain present and version OK
- Fail when toolchain missing
- Fail when version too old
- Suggest installation command

## Test Approach
- Mock toolchain commands where needed
- Test version comparison logic directly
- Use NON_INTERACTIVE=true for consent bypass
- Verify no actual installations occur","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.378100130Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.377592634Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v78","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-yc0","title":"act_run_workflow should pass tag context (GITHUB_REF) to act for release workflows","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-31T02:04:13.415719820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T02:15:17.086295848Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T02:15:17.086067318Z","close_reason":"GITHUB_REF/NAME/TYPE env injection already in act_run_workflow; added unit test to assert tag context","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-z2t","title":"E2E: dsr release (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr release (real behavior)

## Purpose
Validate release upload using real gh auth when available; otherwise skip with guidance.

## Approach
- Use a temp artifacts dir with small files and checksums.
- If gh auth present, target a test repo or draft release.
- If gh auth missing, skip with clear steps.

## Logging
- Log repo, tag, artifacts dir.
- On failure, dump gh output and stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Draft release created and assets uploaded when auth present.
- [ ] Dry-run path produces deterministic plan output.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:55:54.772796976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:34:44.370102301Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:34:44.370085119Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z2t","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:38.810809374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z2t","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:40.415849071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -1208,10 +1208,15 @@ _watch_iteration() {
         fi
     fi
 
-    # Parse check results
+    # Parse check results - extract throttled runs from queued_runs and stuck_runs
+    # JSON structure: .details.repos[].{repo, queued_runs[], stuck_runs[]}
     local throttled_runs
-    throttled_runs=$(echo "$check_output" | jq -r \
-        '.details.repos[]?.throttled_runs[]? | "\(.id)|\(.repo)"' 2>/dev/null)
+    throttled_runs=$(echo "$check_output" | jq -r '
+        .details.repos[]? |
+        . as $r |
+        ((.queued_runs // []) + (.stuck_runs // []))[] |
+        "\(.id)|\($r.repo)"
+    ' 2>/dev/null)
 
     if [[ -z "$throttled_runs" ]]; then
         log_ok "No throttled runs detected"
@@ -1236,18 +1241,22 @@ _watch_iteration() {
                 "$run_id" || true
         fi
 
+        # Extract tool name from repo (e.g., "Dicklesworthstone/ntm" -> "ntm")
+        local tool_name="${repo##*/}"
+
         if $dry_run; then
-            log_info "[DRY RUN] Would trigger fallback for $repo (run $run_id)"
+            log_info "[DRY RUN] Would trigger fallback for $tool_name (run $run_id)"
         else
-            log_info "Triggering fallback for $repo..."
+            log_info "Triggering fallback for $tool_name..."
 
             # Mark as triggered before attempting (to prevent repeated attempts on failure)
             _watch_mark_triggered "$run_id"
 
             # Trigger fallback as a background subprocess
+            # Note: --non-interactive is a global flag and must come before the subcommand
             local fallback_log="$_WATCH_STATE_DIR/fallback-${run_id}.log"
-            log_info "Starting fallback for $repo (run $run_id)..."
-            "$0" fallback "$repo" --non-interactive >> "$fallback_log" 2>&1 &
+            log_info "Starting fallback for $tool_name (run $run_id)..."
+            "$0" --non-interactive fallback "$tool_name" >> "$fallback_log" 2>&1 &
             local fallback_pid=$!
             log_info "Fallback started (PID: $fallback_pid, log: $fallback_log)"
             if [[ "${DSR_NOTIFY_ENABLED:-false}" == "true" ]]; then

@@ -779,7 +779,7 @@ test_live_build_windows_native() {
         return
     fi
     rm -f "$_scp_test"
-    ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=3 wlap 'del C:\tmp\dsr_scp_test 2>nul' &>/dev/null || true
+    # Note: remote /tmp/dsr_scp_test left for OS cleanup (cross-platform rm is complex)
 
     setup_test_environment
